title: Create a Permission Group (Team)
lede: A Permission Group is a team or skill of human agents. Create one, add members, then use its inner tabs to point phone numbers at it and control how it rings. This guide covers creating a group and adding people.

steps.title: Open the Groups tab
steps.content:
1. Go to Call Center → Contact Center.
  2. Click the Groups tab (visible to Contact Center admins and the main account).
  3. The left column lists your existing Permission Groups; the right panel shows the selected group's members and settings.

images.caption: The Groups tab: group list on the left, member and settings panel on the right.
The Groups tab: group list on the left, member and settings panel on the right.

steps.title: Create a new group
steps.content:
1. Click New Group in the top-right.
  2. Enter a Name (for example, Sales Team or Spanish Support).
  3. Optionally add a Description of what the team represents.
  4. Click Create. The new group is selected automatically.

steps.title: Add members to the group
steps.content:
1. With the group selected, open its Members tab.
  2. Click Add Members.
  3. Search users by name or email and pick one or more — both main and sub-accounts can be added.
  4. Click Add. Members appear in the table, each tagged Main or Sub.

To remove someone, click the delete icon on their row and confirm.

images.caption: The Members tab with Add Members and the member list.
The Members tab with Add Members and the member list.

steps.title: Configure the group's inner tabs
steps.content: Once the group has members, its inner tabs let you finish setup: Members Roles, Role Mapping, Inbound SIP Routing, Ring strategy, Wait & Hold experience, and Queue Settings. Each has its own Save button — start with Members Roles and Inbound SIP Routing.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: Assign roles and route calls to your new group.

cards.title: Members & Roles
cards.description: Set each member's Contact Center role.

cards.title: Inbound SIP Routing
cards.description: Point a phone number at this group.
